---
title: "Episode 5: Best January Transfer Signings Ever? — Can Stats Decide?"
date: 2026-01-27
description: "Who is the best January transfer signing ever? We take a look at some of the big names and see if stats can settle the debate."
categories: [Football, Premier League, Transfers, Elo, Changepoint Analysis]
---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-6}
**Published**: 2026-01-27  
**Duration**: 33:45  
**YouTube**: [Watch here](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fEXpzZSyGoI)
:::

::: {.g-col-6}
**Topics Covered**: Football, Premier League, January Transfers, Elo Ratings, Changepoint Analysis
:::

:::

## Video

<iframe width="100%" height="400" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/fEXpzZSyGoI" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## Overview

Welcome to the Corridor of Uncertainty, a podcast at the intersection of maths & sport. Hosted by Dr Jess Hargreaves & Dr Rich Bingham, two mathematicians at the University of York.

Who is the best January transfer signing ever? We take a look at some of the big names and see if we can use stats to settle the debate.

---

**Timestamps:**

- 2:12 Honourable Mentions
- 6:50 Vidic vs van Dijk
- 10:20 Elo ratings
- 13:40 Changepoint analysis
- 17:10 Vidic's Change Points
- 21:55 van Dijk's Change Points
- 25:30 Before January vs After January
- 28:28 Newcastle's Incredible Turnaround
- 30:31 The correct answer!

**References:**

- <https://www.premierleague.com/en/news...>
- <https://www.fourfourtwo.com/features/...>
- Killick, R., & Eckley, I. A. (2014). changepoint: An R package for changepoint analysis. *Journal of Statistical Software*, 58, 1–19.

## Key Concepts

::: {.callout-tip}
## Elo Ratings
\[Explanation of how Elo ratings work in a football context\]
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Changepoint Analysis
\[Explanation of changepoint analysis and how it detects structural breaks in performance data\]
:::

## Resources & References

- \[Link to paper/article 1\]
- \[Link to code repository\]
- \[Link to related resource\]

## Transcript Highlights

> "\[Notable quote from the video\]"

\[Additional context or explanation\]

---

> "\[Another notable quote\]"

\[Context\]

## Code Examples

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Python

```python
# Example code from episode
def example_function():
    pass
```

## R

```r
# R equivalent if applicable
example_function <- function() {
  # code
}
```

:::

## Discussion

\[Add any additional commentary, extended explanations, or connections to other episodes\]

::: {.callout-note}
## Related Episodes
- \[Episode X: Related Topic\](episode-00X.qmd)
- \[Episode Y: Follow-up Topic\](episode-00Y.qmd)
:::
